title: Listening - Part 2
description: For each question, choose the correct answer.
instructions: You will hear six short extracts. For questions 8–13, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear.

extracts.context: You will hear two people talking about a train journey.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: Was your train journey okay yesterday? || Man: Not really. I got a seat, and it left on time, so I thought everything would be fine. [8]But then there was a problem with the doors at one station, and we had to wait there for nearly forty minutes[/8]. || Woman: That's annoying, especially when the journey should be short. || Woman: I might ask someone else as well, just to be sure, but at least I have a much better idea now.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Was your train journey okay yesterday?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Not really. I got a seat, and it left on time, so I thought everything would be fine. But then there was a problem with the doors at one station, and we had to wait there for nearly forty minutes.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: That's annoying, especially when the journey should be short.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I might ask someone else as well, just to be sure, but at least I have a much better idea now.

questions.question: Why was the man's journey delayed?

options.A: There was a problem with the train doors.
options.B: The train left the first station late.
options.C: He could not find a seat.

questions.explanation: He says there was a problem with the doors and they had to wait for nearly forty minutes.

extracts.context: You will hear a woman talking about a new hobby.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Are you still going to pottery classes? || Woman: Yes, and I'm enjoying them. I thought making bowls would be simple, but it takes practice. The teacher shows us slowly, which helps. [9]I don't mind the cost, but carrying the finished pieces home on the bus is awkward[/9]. || Man: Maybe I can drive you next week. || Woman: Well, I hope it works out. It sounds as if you have thought about it carefully, which is always a sensible start.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Are you still going to pottery classes?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Yes, and I'm enjoying them. I thought making bowls would be simple, but it takes practice. The teacher shows us slowly, which helps. I don't mind the cost, but carrying the finished pieces home on the bus is awkward.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Maybe I can drive you next week.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Well, I hope it works out. It sounds as if you have thought about it carefully, which is always a sensible start.

questions.question: What does the woman find difficult about the pottery class?
questions.explanation: She says carrying the finished pieces home on the bus is awkward.

extracts.context: You will hear two friends talking about a new café.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: Have you tried the café next to the cinema? || Man: Yes, I went there before the film. The coffee was good, and the cakes looked amazing, but the queue was so long. [10]I nearly missed the start of the film because the service was really slow[/10]. || Woman: Oh, I hate waiting when I'm in a hurry. || Woman: Maybe next time it will be simpler. Things like this often seem more difficult before you know the full story.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Have you tried the café next to the cinema?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Yes, I went there before the film. The coffee was good, and the cakes looked amazing, but the queue was so long. I nearly missed the start of the film because the service was really slow.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Oh, I hate waiting when I'm in a hurry.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Maybe next time it will be simpler. Things like this often seem more difficult before you know the full story.

questions.question: What problem did the man have at the café?

options.C: He had to wait too long.

questions.explanation: He says the service was really slow and he nearly missed the start of the film.

extracts.context: You will hear a girl telling a friend about a music festival.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Did you enjoy the music festival? || Girl: Yes, although it was smaller than last year. A few bands cancelled because of the weather. [11]But my favourite singer still performed, and that's the main reason I bought a ticket[/11]. || Man: So you weren't too disappointed then? || Girl: No, not really. I had a great time. || Man: I agree. It is worth taking a little time over it, instead of rushing and regretting the choice later.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Did you enjoy the music festival?

dialogue.speaker: girl
dialogue.text: Yes, although it was smaller than last year. A few bands cancelled because of the weather. But my favourite singer still performed, and that's the main reason I bought a ticket.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: So you weren't too disappointed then?

dialogue.speaker: girl
dialogue.text: No, not really. I had a great time.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I agree. It is worth taking a little time over it, instead of rushing and regretting the choice later.

questions.question: Why was the girl pleased with the festival?

options.A: She saw the singer she wanted to see.
options.B: It was bigger than last year's festival.
options.C: The weather was better than expected.

questions.explanation: She says her favourite singer still performed, which was the main reason she bought a ticket.

extracts.context: You will hear two colleagues talking about a meeting room.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: Is the small meeting room free this afternoon? || Man: It is, but I wouldn't use it for the client meeting. The projector works now, and there are enough chairs. [12]The problem is that the room gets very hot after lunch, and there's no window to open[/12]. || Woman: Thanks for warning me. I'll book the larger room instead. || Woman: That makes sense. Sometimes the practical details are more important than they seem at the beginning.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Is the small meeting room free this afternoon?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: It is, but I wouldn't use it for the client meeting. The projector works now, and there are enough chairs. The problem is that the room gets very hot after lunch, and there's no window to open.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Thanks for warning me. I'll book the larger room instead.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: That makes sense. Sometimes the practical details are more important than they seem at the beginning.

questions.question: Why does the man advise against using the small room?
questions.explanation: The man says the room gets very hot after lunch and has no window to open.

extracts.context: You will hear a woman talking about her neighbour's dog.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Is your neighbour's dog still causing problems? || Woman: Not like before. It used to bark early in the morning, but they've trained it better. It doesn't come into my garden now either. [13]The only issue is that it jumps up at people in the hallway, which can be frightening[/13]. || Man: You should mention it politely. || Woman: I can understand that. It is always useful when someone explains the situation clearly before you decide what to do.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Is your neighbour's dog still causing problems?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Not like before. It used to bark early in the morning, but they've trained it better. It doesn't come into my garden now either. The only issue is that it jumps up at people in the hallway, which can be frightening.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: You should mention it politely.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I can understand that. It is always useful when someone explains the situation clearly before you decide what to do.

questions.question: What problem does the woman still have with the dog?
questions.explanation: She says the only issue is that the dog jumps up at people in the hallway.
